title: The Phone of the Wind: Whispers to Lost
subTitle: A phone booth in a private garden in northern Japan gives hope to people who lost loved ones in the 2011 earthquake and tsunami.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Running Across Recovering Sanriku
subTitle: A group of runners prepare for a stage race held on the coast of Iwate, covering 80 miles in three days. They learn about the area's recovery, thirteen years after the 2011 Great East Japan Earthquake and Tsunami.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Anime Manga Explosion
subTitle: Discovers the secrets of Japanese anime and manga as their popularity continues to grow. Considers the appeal of these Japanese productions internationally, how they're created, and their cultural significance.

title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
subTitle: Curly and his friends find a rare caterpillar during their outdoor time and decide to take care of it. However, Curly argues with them as the others become too carefree.

title: Texico
subTitle: Fascinating experiments that help people visualise sequences and predict future outcomes. Conveys the pleasure found in improving sequence patterns and how programming principles can be discovered in many aspects of daily life.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: J-MELO
subTitle: CROWN HEAD, a rock band creating a buzz after debuting this year, and Takagi Reni from Momoiro Clover Z stop by the studio to chat and perform.

title: Little Charo in Tohoku
subTitle: Charo and his companions go on a journey in the Tohoku region to search for the Eight-Starred Note that can stop the monster from reappearing.

title: Japanology Plus Mini
subTitle: Presenting a variety of short conceptual scenarios focusing on the different ways of life in and around Japan.

title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
subTitle: Ricky was born in an apple orchard. It's a perfect playground for mischievous kittens! Pep spends the autumn in golden rice fields, hiding and hunting grasshoppers. Watch out for his pounce!

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Hit the Road
subTitle: NHK WORLD-JAPAN presents a wide range of programs from news, current affairs, documentaries, etc. focusing on Japan and Asia.

title: Core Kyoto Mini
subTitle: A look at the timeless heart of Japan's ancient capital. Against its rich backdrop of culture and tradition, today's Kyoto continues to innovate and inspire, even after being established 1,200 years ago.

title: Easy Japanese for Work
subTitle: Irving-san, from Mexico, has trouble with keego. Soon, he will begin working at the clients' office two days a week. To improve his keego, Irving-san will tackle the role-play challenge.

title: Zero Waste Life
subTitle: With falling demand and fewer occasions to wear kimonos, Tajiri Daichi turns discarded kimono fabric into stylish shoes, bringing tradition into everyday life and helping to keep it alive.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Somewhere Street
subTitle: A unique walking-eye view of the greatest cities around the globe. Featuring chats with locals and enjoying encounters that only strolling the city streets can bring.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
subTitle: Okinawa Prefecture is a group of subtropical islands in the far south of Japan. We examine its past and present, with footage from time-lapse creator Shimizu Daisuke. This time: Shuri Castle.

title: Grand Sumo Highlights
subTitle: Featuring all the top-division bouts from 14 September 2025, the first day of the Grand Sumo Tournament in Tokyo.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with information on the techniques, traditions and famous wrestlers of the past. The rules may be simple but there is a lot more to sumo than meets the eye.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Chatroom Japan
subTitle: Xu Quanyi sees it as his mission to promote the charm of Fukushima. Sharing his stories is how he counters the stigma Fukushima continues to suffer following the 2011 disaster.

title: IMPACTS:Climates Change the World
subTitle: Experts use scientific data and the latest discoveries to analyse the impact of global warming and explore potential solutions.

title: Design Museum Japan
subTitle: To make a dantsu carpet, everything from yarn-making to weaving is done by hand. Minagawa experiences the deep allure of handmade goods.

title: Where We Call Home
subTitle: People from around the globe who put down roots in Japan offer a glimpse into their lives and the hardships they face in Japanese society.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
subTitle: One of the greatest mysteries that archaeologists are trying to unveil is that of the stone circles. Ceremonial stone circles first appeared at the beginning of Jomon era. Hundreds of stone circles and stone features have cropped up all over Japan.

title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life
subTitle: A look at Japan's efforts to improve tsunami evacuation, from overcoming normalcy bias that causes people to underestimate it's risks, to the installation of coastal evacuation towers.

title: Magical Japanese
subTitle: Focusing on words related to yama (mountains). Mountains make up about 75 per cent of Japan's territory, and they have made their way into many common expressions in the Japanese language.

title: Easy Japanese
subTitle: To express an impression of something, one has to use any adjective that ends with i, like kawaii, followed by the name of the object, and desu ne.

title: Culture Crossroads
subTitle: Ukrainian musician Kateryna talks about her motivation to support displaced people in Japan after Russia's invasion of her homeland.

title: Hello! NHK WORLD-JAPAN
subTitle: Explores various aspects of Hokkaido, ranging from the ecology of brown bears and the craftsmanship of a bespoke bento to a landscape uniquely shaped by the region's rugged natural conditions and pioneering history.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Seasoning the Seasons
subTitle: Explores the history of the Seto Inland Sea in western Japan, famous for its abundance of stones, and about the traditions stemming from its stones, including stone baths and a musical instrument called sanukite.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
subTitle: Born yesterday has a very different meaning for Mandarin ducklings in the big city. Their second day of life is a rite-of-passage, avoiding crows and man-made hazards in search of the nearest pond.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
subTitle: Shimizu Daisuke investigates various aspects of Japanese culture and traditions using the technique of time-lapse photography.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Hometown Stories
subTitle: Intimate portraits of people from around Japan, each leading diverse lives while enriching lives of others, with themes ranging from ancient traditions to contemporary social issues.

subTitle: Sanma is a fish that shines like a sword. In Japan, it's popular and reasonably-priced at around a dollar per fish. We visit Kesennuma, a sanma port in Tohoku, to learn about this autumn treat.

subTitle: For a time during the Edo period, fireworks lit up the skies practically every night in the summer. It was a chance for women to dress up and let loose and grab the attention of male suitors.

subTitle: Wagyu captivates food lovers worldwide with fine marbling, melt-in-the-mouth texture, and rich aroma. Join us as we explore strict cattle management and the dishes that highlight wagyu's appeal.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: Visiting an Edo-style open-air teahouse, where the townspeople enjoy refreshments, conversation, and the company of exotic birds. For them, these outings were about more than a good time.

title: Newsroom Tokyo
subTitle: Comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia. It explains the day's developments and what is behind them.

title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Ito Junji is a horror manga artist renowned worldwide for his intricate artwork and unique vision, which earned him a place in the prestigious Eisner Awards Hall of Fame in 2025. What inspires him?

title: Japan's Top Inventions
subTitle: Nikki Tsukamoto Kininmonth learns about heat-resistant labels and meets Takehisa Kenji, the CEO of a company that manufactures these labels.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Frontrunners
subTitle: Social entrepreneur Doya Yuto supports Ghana through his fashion brand and NPO. By providing fair wages and reinvesting profits, he builds schools and expands education in disadvantaged communities.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: Japanese yams are a traditional ingredient loved for their sticky texture. Experience a harvest using heavy machinery to dig deep underground, then savor dishes including tororo over rice.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Dive in Tokyo
subTitle: Okachimachi is an area just north of Akihabara renowned for its jewelry district. Its story involves Buddhist craftspeople, American servicemen, and more recently, communities of foreign artisans.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Kabuki Kool
subTitle: The historic figure of Minamoto no Yoshitsune has been immortalised in many kabuki plays as a child, a skilled warrior, and a tragic hero. Explore with actor Kataoka Ainosuke.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: Every round of sumo wrestling requires a sacred ritual to expel the evil spirits, including drinking water to cleanse and purify their bodies.
